description: Tasks rewritten to match actual generated data

tasks.instruction: Find open youth- or education-focused grants with minimum awards of at least $5,000 and application deadlines between May and December 2025, then save the one with the highest maximum award to your saved grants list.
tasks.name: Find open youth- or education-focused grants with minimum awards of at least $5,000 and application deadlines between May and December 2025, then save the one with the highest maximum award to your saved grants list.
tasks.description: Find open youth- or education-focused grants with minimum awards of at least $5,000 and application deadlines between May and December 2025, then save the one with the highest maximum award to your saved grants list.

tasks.instruction: Create a new organization account for a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, set a modest six-figure annual budget, and select exactly the Environment and Neighborhoods program areas before saving the profile.
tasks.name: Create a new organization account for a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, set a modest six-figure annual budget, and select exactly the Environment and Neighborhoods program areas before saving the profile.
tasks.description: Create a new organization account for a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, set a modest six-figure annual budget, and select exactly the Environment and Neighborhoods program areas before saving the profile.

tasks.instruction: Create an individual applicant account, find neighborhood-focused grants sorted by lowest maximum award first, start an application for a grant whose maximum award is no more than $5,000, fill in the basic project information, and save the application as a draft.
tasks.name: Create an individual applicant account, find neighborhood-focused grants sorted by lowest maximum award first, start an application for a grant whose maximum award is no more than $5,000, fill in the basic project information, and save the application as a draft.

tasks.instruction: Browse arts & culture grants sorted by soonest deadline, open the first two results, compare their required match percentages, and save to your application plan the one with the lower match requirement.
tasks.name: Browse arts & culture grants sorted by soonest deadline, open the first two results, compare their required match percentages, and save to your application plan the one with the lower match requirement.
tasks.description: Browse arts & culture grants sorted by soonest deadline, open the first two results, compare their required match percentages, and save to your application plan the one with the lower match requirement.

tasks.instruction: From past grants, filter to health-related awards made in 2023 between $10,000 and $25,000 and save a comparison of the first three results.
tasks.name: From past grants, filter to health-related awards made in 2023 between $10,000 and $25,000 and save a comparison of the first three results.
tasks.description: From past grants, filter to health-related awards made in 2023 between $10,000 and $25,000 and save a comparison of the first three results.

tasks.instruction: Sign up for grant email alerts using countywide geographic focus, select Youth & Families and Environment as your interests, choose monthly emails, and enable email but not SMS notifications.
tasks.name: Sign up for grant email alerts using countywide geographic focus, select Youth & Families and Environment as your interests, choose monthly emails, and enable email but not SMS notifications.
tasks.description: Sign up for grant email alerts using countywide geographic focus, select Youth & Families and Environment as your interests, choose monthly emails, and enable email but not SMS notifications.

tasks.instruction: Use the website's contact form to send an eligibility question about the foundation’s flagship flexible community grant that supports education, youth, health, and neighborhood vitality across the county, selecting “Eligibility Question” as the topic and choosing email as the preferred response method.
tasks.name: Use the website's contact form to send an eligibility question about the foundation’s flagship flexible community grant that supports education, youth, health, and neighborhood vitality across the county, selecting “Eligibility Question” as the topic and choosing email as the preferred response method.

tasks.instruction: For an individual applicant account, open notification settings, turn on email deadline reminders 14 days before grant due dates, keep email notifications for grant updates enabled, and turn off email notifications for news or blog posts before saving.
